title: paspalum pennatum
intro: Paspalum pennatum, also known as East Indian grass or buffalo grass, is a perennial grass native to the tropical and subtropical regions of the world. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe skin irritations, wounds, and infections. The health benefits of Paspalum pennatum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Paspalum pennatum can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used as a poultice to apply directly to the skin for its medicinal benefits.
